tex: Write the number six hundred and seven thousand five hundred and thirty-two in figures.

From the list of numbers, write down
61 \quad 62 \quad 63 \quad 64 \quad 65 \quad 66 \quad 67 \quad 68 \quad 69
(a) a square number,

(b) a multiple of 13,

(c) a factor of 186,

(d) the prime numbers.

The stem-and-leaf diagram shows the journey time to school of some students.
\begin{tabular}{r|l}
1 & 3\;5\;7\;9\;9 \\
2 & 3\;4\;5 \\
3 & 0\;3\;4\;6\;7 \\
4 & 2\;4\;5\;8
\end{tabular}
Key: $1 \mid 3$ represents 13 minutes
Find
(a) the number of students with a journey time of more than 35 minutes,

(b) the mode.
texHtml:
The stem-and-leaf diagram shows the journey time to school of some students.
13\;5\;7\;9\;9
23\;4\;5
30\;3\;4\;6\;7
42\;4\;5\;8
Key: $1 \mid 3$ represents 13 minutes Find (a) the number of students with a journey time of more than 35 minutes, (b) the mode.

tex:
This is Arania's method to divide $213$ by $12\frac{1}{2}$ without using a calculator.
\[
\begin{aligned}
213 \div 12\frac{1}{2} &= 426 \div 25 \\
&= 852 \div 50 \\
&= 1704 \div 100 \\
&= 17.04
\end{aligned}
\]
Show how to use Arania's method to work out $135 \div 12\frac{1}{2}$ without using a calculator.

Put one pair of brackets into each calculation to make it correct.

(a) $6 \times 7 - 5 + 4 = 16$

(b) $-2^2 + 24 \div 12 - 4 = 2$

At noon, the temperature is $4^\circ\text{C}$.
At midnight, the temperature is $-9^\circ\text{C}$.

Work out the difference in temperature between noon and midnight.

Thibault records the number of cars of each colour in a car park.
\begin{tabular}{|l|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Colour & Black & White & Silver & Red \\
\hline
Number of cars & 8 & 5 & 4 & 3 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(a) He draws a pie chart to show this information.\\
Calculate the sector angle for the red cars.

(b) Two more white cars enter the car park and no cars leave the car park.\\
When these two white cars are included in the results, will the sector angle for the red cars change?\\
Without doing any further calculations, give a reason for your decision.

 because

tex:
Let $p = \left(\dfrac{2}{8}\right)$ and $q = \left(\dfrac{-1}{4}\right)$.
Find
(a) $p - q$,

(b) $6p$.
Find the total surface area of a cuboid with length $8$ cm, width $6$ cm and height $3$ cm.

(a) The total cost of $n$ bags of flour is \$d.\\
Write down an expression for the cost of one bag of flour.

\$ 

(b) A bag of rice costs \$r and a bag of almonds costs \$a.\\
Pedro buys $x$ bags of rice and $y$ bags of almonds.\\
Write down an expression for the change that Pedro receives from a \$20 note.

\$

(b) Find the value of $6789^{\frac{1}{3}}$.\\

\[
\frac{27 - 2.3^2}{845.4 \times 0.048}.
\]

The length, $l$ metres, of a piece of rope is $30.7$ m, correct to 1 decimal place.\\
Complete this statement about the value of $l$.

$3(2a - b) - b$

(b) Factorise.\\
$x^2 - 8xy$
Find the lowest common multiple (LCM) of 24 and 28.

Without using a calculator, work out $4\dfrac{1}{8} - 2\dfrac{5}{6}$.\\
You must show all your working and give your answer as a mixed number in its simplest form.
